name: Amr ibn Muta'
title: Epic Reciter
shortDescription: A warrior from the Ju'fi tribe who bravely went to the battlefield in Karbala and was martyred after reciting epic poetry.
fullStory:
Amr ibn Muta' al-Ju'fi was another hero from the Ju'fi tribe in Kufa who, fully aware of the path of truth, joined Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan.

On Ashura, after the martyrdom of many companions, Amr ibn Muta' sought permission to fight with Haidari bravery. He entered the battlefield and recited epic poetry that sent shivers down the enemies' spines.

In his poems, he said: 'I am the son of Muta' and today I defend Hussain with my sword...' With high combat skills, he attacked the Kufan ranks, bringing down several of them with every strike of his sword.

Finally, after a long battle and exhausting the enemies, he was surrounded by Umar Sa'd's army and, with numerous wounds, attained the grace of martyrdom.
quote: (In his war poetry:) I am the son of Muta' and my sword is drawn; today I defend Hussain.
